t_ch: 这么说吧，关于「再创世」究竟意味着什么，恐怕就连一直信奉鼓吹它的黄金裔们，也都无法给出一个令人满意的答案哦。
t_en: The Era Nova? Let's say even the Chrysos Heirs who preach about it can't properly explain what it truly means.

t_ch: 「绝对的完美」、「永恒的幸福」…这些虚假的概念就像劣质的大地兽玩偶，只能为走投无路的人们提供些许聊胜于无的慰藉。
t_en: "Absolute perfection," "Eternal bliss"... Empty concepts like shoddy dromas plushies — comforting only the desperate.

t_ch: 虽然我对此还挺好奇的，但也仅此而已啦。比起这个，我对你们讨伐天空泰坦的经历，反倒更感兴趣些呢…那么大的宫殿究竟是怎么盖在天上的？难道说世界本无天地之分？
t_en: Curious? Certainly. But I'm more intrigued by your Sky Titan conquests... How does one build such an enormous palace in the sky? Does that mean that the sky and earth are not separated?

t_ch: 我先走了，再见。
t_en: I'll take my leave now.

t_ch: 唉，美妙的时光总是如此短暂…希望我们还有缘再见，朋友！
t_en: Ah, how fleeting sweet moments are... Till fate reunites us, friend!

t_ch: {F#女士}{M#先生}好呀！这段时间我在跟着达利安大叔强身健体。他一直在守护圣城公民们的安全，我虽然个头小，也能用自己的方式守护大家。
t_en: Good day, {F#miss}{M#mister}! I've been training with Uncle Dorian lately. While he protects the holy city, I'll guard everyone in my own way — even if I'm small!

t_ch: 是你啊，{F#女士}{M#先生}，这些日子，我又学会了不少字。
t_en: Oh, it's you, {F#Miss}{M#Mister}... I've learned many new words recently.

t_ch: 克帖西芬姐姐说，识的字越多，越有希望在来世成为大司铎、替大家解读神谕…虽然我不觉得神谕需要专人解读，但我会往那个方向努力的。
t_en: Ctesiphona says literacy improves one's chances of becoming a High Priestess in the afterlife to interpret the prophecy... Though I don't see why edicts need interpreting, I'll still try my best!

t_ch: {F#女士}{M#先生}，你喜欢水漫琴的歌声吗？
t_en: {F#Miss}{M#Mister}, do you like the sound of the Water Lyre?

t_ch: 弥斯姐姐的弹奏，总是让我回想起第一次见到刻法勒的那天。如果我能把这份感动的心情写成歌唱出来就好了……
t_en: Misia's performance always reminds me of the day I first encountered Kephale. If only I could turn this moving emotion into a song...

t_ch: 啊！{F#女士}{M#先生}，我和阿芙罗姐姐学了很久的缝纫，但总觉得这项技能不太适合我。
t_en: Ah, {F#miss}{M#mister}! I've studied sewing with Aphrea for ages, but it never quite suited me.

t_ch: 所以我常常偷溜出来，做点别的事情，好在阿芙罗姐姐对我很宽松，尤其是她知道我在云崖上的经历之后……
t_en: So I often sneak out to do other things. Luckily, Aphrea's lenient, especially after learning about my time at Dawncloud...

t_ch: {F#女士}{M#先生}你来啦？现在的我已经不再到处逃难了，奥赫玛就是我最后的家园，我想用自己的力量守护它。
t_en: {F#Miss}{M#Mister}, you're here. I don't wander as a refugee anymore – Okhema's my final home. I'll protect it with my own strength.

t_ch: 你的两个小伙伴去哪儿啦？
t_en: Where are your two little companions?

t_ch: 你还记得他们呀，亚森和拉缇娜，那两个小鬼，现在都可听话了！
t_en: You remember them! Yason and Lathena – both rascals behave properly now!

t_ch: 一个不再天天担心会被陨石砸死，另一个在安全区跑前跑后，帮助来避难的人们。
t_en: One stopped worrying about meteor strikes, the other runs around the safe zone helping refugees.

t_ch: 这都多亏了我们在云崖听到的「刻法勒」神谕，你说是吧？
t_en: All thanks to Kephale's prophecy we heard at Dawncloud, right?

t_ch: 嘘——我们一起保守这个秘密。
t_en: Shh— let's keep this secret together.

t_ch: 嘘——当然！我只悄悄和你一个人说，现在市集里的叔叔阿姨、哥哥姐姐，还有小鬼头们，都相信我真的听到了天父的神谕。
t_en: Shh— Of course! I'll whisper it only to you: now every adult and child at the market believes I truly heard the sky father's prophecy.

t_ch: 每当有人问我：希瑞雅，希瑞雅，这样的日子什么时候是个头，我们到底要怎么办？我就说：安心等待就好啦，刻法勒一定会回应我们的信仰。
t_en: When they ask "Serena, Serena, when will this end? What should we do?" I say: "Wait peacefully – Kephale will answer our faith."

t_ch: 这不算撒谎，对吗？拉个勾，我们一起保护大家心中希望的小火苗吧。
t_en: That's not lying, is it? Pinky promise – we'll protect the tiny spark of hope in everyone's heart.

t_ch: 刻法勒的小祭司，你好啊！
t_en: Hello, little priestess of Kephale!

t_ch: 嘿嘿，被{F#女士}{M#先生}你这么称呼，还有点不好意思。
t_en: Hehe, {F#miss}{M#mister}, that title makes me blush!

t_ch: 你瞧，这儿是整个市集最开阔的地方，好多来奥赫玛避难的人都会从这儿经过，包括…从阿卡索斯第一次来奥赫玛的我。
t_en: Look, this is the most open place in the whole market. Many people who come to seek shelter in Okhema pass through here. Including... me, who came here from Icatus.

t_ch: 只不过，现在末日降临，又多了不少想从圣城离开，去其他城邦避难的人。
t_en: But now that the apocalypse is coming, many more people want to leave here and seek shelter in other cities.

t_ch: 我站在这里，是为了提醒他们——逃难是没有尽头的，不如安心留在圣城。
t_en: I stand here to remind them – fleeing has no end. Better to stay peacefully in the holy city.

t_ch: 奥赫玛是我们最后要守护、也最终会庇护我们的家园，一定是的。
t_en: Okhema is the last home we'll protect... and will ultimately shelter us. It must be.

t_ch: 现在你怎么看待「再创世」？
t_en: What do you think of the "Era Nova" now?

t_ch: {F#女士}{M#先生}担心我觉得「再创世」是好心的卫兵叔叔、还有你，一起给像我这样的孩子们编织的安慰吗？
t_en: {F#Miss}{M#Mister}, you worry I think "Era Nova" is just kind guards and you weaving comforting tales for children like me?

t_ch: 恰恰相反，我明白了，刻法勒虽然不再对人们说话，但它的庇护一刻也没有停止过。
t_en: On the contrary – I've realized Kephale's protection never ceased, though it no longer speaks to us.

t_ch: 如果不是命运保佑，我一定早早死在淹没阿卡索斯的黑潮里了。不会有机会来到圣城脚下，遇见照顾我的好心人们，也不敢想象有一天我能去黎明云崖。
t_en: Without destiny's mercy, I'd have died in Icatus' black tide. Never reached the holy city, met kind caretakers, nor dared dream of visiting Dawncloud.

t_ch: 连听到「神谕」这样的巧合，也是因为天父心怀仁慈，愿意让我怀抱着希望，并把希望传递给大家……
t_en: Even hearing the prophecy was sky father's mercy – letting me cradle hope and pass it onward...

t_ch: 所以，别为我担心！说不定第二天睁开眼，我们就看到重新亮起来的天空了呢。
t_en: So don't worry! Maybe tomorrow we'll wake to see the sky relit.

t_ch: 加油，小机灵鬼。
t_en: Keep it up, smart one.

t_ch: 我会的，我是被「刻法勒」眷顾的希瑞雅。
t_en: I will – I'm Serena, blessed by Kephale!

t_ch: 也期待和{F#女士}{M#先生}你在新世界再次相遇哦！
t_en: {F#Miss}{M#Mister}, let's meet again in the new world!

t_ch: 亲爱的帕门弥德，事到如今我不愿再用离奇的问题来刁难你，我只问一句：你怎么不跑呢？
t_en: Dear Parmenides, I won't plague you with strange questions anymore — just one: Why don't you flee?

t_ch: 希诺啊，我的老朋友，看在你竟也会关心人的份上，我就回答你：我相信灵魂不朽，它能在再创世后为我们找到新的躯体。你又为什么不跑呢？
t_en: Ha, Zeno, my old friend! Since you show concern, I'll answer: I believe souls endure, finding new vessels in the Era Nova. Why don't you flee?

t_ch: 与你恰恰相反，我相信灵魂与心灵同样依靠躯体存在，身死灵魂即告消散，所以再跑也毫无意义。
t_en: Contrary to you, I think souls depend on flesh – death scatters both. Fleeing's meaningless.

t_ch: 热爱智慧的听众啊，你又为什么不跑呢？
t_en: Oh, wise listener – why don't you flee?

t_ch: 我不怕。
t_en: I fear nothing.

t_ch: 还能跑哪儿去呢？
t_en: Where can I run to?

t_ch: 差点忘了，我这就跑。
t_en: Oh, I almost forgot. I'll start fleeing now.

t_ch: 那么，不妨就来听听我们这两位修辞学教师最后的论辩吧。如此大错特错的观点，可不能任由它流毒到来世。
t_en: Then let us hear the final debate between these two rhetoric teachers. Such gravely mistaken views mustn't be allowed to poison the afterlife.

t_ch: 我必须申明，灵魂是形式，躯体不过是承载灵魂的质料。假若灵魂并不存在，那么我现在在同什么对话？你的感觉、思维又寄宿于何处，你的行动又由何而起？
t_en: I must declare that the soul is but a form, and the body is merely its material vessel. If souls don't exist, what converses with me now? Where do your senses dwell, your thoughts reside, your actions originate?

t_ch: 答案恰恰就在躯体之中啊，帕门弥德。我的耳倾听了你的声音，我的眼看到了你的样貌，当你说蠢话时，我面颊上的血液变得更加灼热，也为你感到害臊呢。
t_en: The answer lies precisely within the flesh, Parmenides! My ears hear your voice, my eyes behold your form. When you spout nonsense, blood burns my cheeks with secondhand shame.

t_ch: 可如此说来，常有祭司和黄金裔可得见灵魂，你如何解释呢？
t_en: Yet priests and the Chrysos Heirs often claim to see souls. How would you explain this?

t_ch: 是啊…
t_en: Yeah...

t_ch: 这个世界观是能看到灵魂的。
t_en: This worldview does allow us to see souls.

t_ch: 我也见过。
t_en: I've seen them too.

t_ch: 无知无觉，了无回应，那不过是过往的倒影，和你在镜中见到的自己并无二致。
t_en: Those unfeeling, unresponsive reflections of the past differ not from your own reflection in the mirror.

t_ch: 不可分割的微粒构成了我们的世界，也组成了我们的躯体。它们的运动、排列和组合并非无限，而是有迹可循的。
t_en: Indivisible particles compose our world and bodies. Their movements, order and combinations follow patterns, not infinite possibilities.

t_ch: 那些思考、欲念和行动——我们所谓灵魂的东西，只不过是诸多可能性中被规定好的一种。
t_en: Thoughts, desires, actions — what we call "soul" is but predetermined permutations of possibilities.

t_ch: 如果你被规定好永远只能思考着这些无稽的念头，未免也太可怜了。假使灵魂不存在，你口中的可能性又是由谁来规定的呢？是谁为你选择了命运？
t_en: How pitiful if you're fated to entertain such absurd notions! If souls don't exist, who dictates these possibilities? Who chooses our fates?

t_ch: 神明？
t_en: The gods?

t_ch: 他自己？
t_en: Ourselves?

t_ch: 随机的？
t_en: Or just by random chance?

t_ch: 不，正是灵魂！灵魂作为形式，是我们存在、行动的原因和目的。它决定了我们是何物，引导躯体潜在的存在实现，使之能够成为我们自己。
t_en: No — the soul! As a form, it's the cause and purpose of our existence and actions. It defines our being, actualizing the body's latent potential to become ourselves.

t_ch: 这更可笑了——看看四周吧，世界在破碎，时间也早已脱轨。而我们呢，在诡辩中等死。你的灵魂一定喝蜜酿喝昏头了，才想要实现自己的这样的潜能。
t_en: Preposterous! Look around — the world shatters, time derails. Yet here we await death through sophistry. Your soul must be pickled in honey brew to seek such actualization!

t_ch: 你才喝昏头了呢！先天形式不是香皂，吹不出五颜六色的肥皂泡。你要多放点盐，口感才够硬。
t_en: Yours is pickled! The natural form isn't like soap, it will not produce colorful bubbles. Add more salt to harden your reasoning.

t_ch: 你也配对我指手画脚？我刚修剪过指甲，所以上次理发掉了不少头发。你要是惹我，可算是踢到了大地兽！
t_en: You dare lecture me? My trimmed nails caused hair loss during last haircut. Provoke me, and I'll show you the wrath of a dromas!

t_ch: …你们在说什么？
t_en: ...What are you guys talking about?

t_ch: 不要吵架，不要吵架。
t_en: No fighting, please.

t_ch: 善语结善缘，恶言伤人心。
t_en: Kind words form friendly bonds, and harsh ones break the heart.

t_ch: 啊，怎么回事，我们…怎么在说乱七八糟的话？
t_en: Ah, what's happening? Why are we talking... such nonsense?

t_ch: 恐怕是因为{RUBY_B#理性之泰坦}瑟希斯{RUBY_E#}的陨落，理性和逻辑也变得不再稳固了。
t_en: Likely due to {RUBY_B#Reason Titan}Cerces{RUBY_E#}' fall, reason and logic has grown unstable.

t_ch: 唉，那我们还争辩些什么呢？事已至此，我们再没有地方可去了。
t_en: Then why debate? At this point, we have nowhere left to go.

t_ch: 是啊，没有地方可去。
t_en: Indeed, nowhere.

t_ch: 唯有这点是确凿无疑的。
t_en: Only this remains certain.

t_ch: 阁下，这里是抵抗黑潮的前线，万勿再继续向前了。
t_en: My lord, this is the frontline against the black tide. Advance no further.

t_ch: 你怎么在这里？
t_en: Why are you here?

t_ch: 黑潮造物冲垮了防线。我和探险队赶来支援时，此处的惨状难以言喻。
t_en: Black tide creations breached defenses. When our expedition came to aid... the carnage defied description.

t_ch: 直到市集中的卫兵和工匠赶来支援，才勉强把防线稳固在这里。
t_en: We only managed to secure this position when guards and craftsmen rushed over from the marketplace to reinforce us.

t_ch: 驻守城门的卫队长…不幸在黑潮中罹难，完成了天父交给他的使命。现在，这里由我指挥。
t_en: The captain of the city gate... fell to the black tide, fulfilling his duty to the sky father. I've taken command of our forces here now.

t_ch: 情况如何？
t_en: How's the situation?

t_ch: 黑潮已经被击退，但每隔一阵子就会有新的造物来袭。虽然还能勉力支撑，但更可怕的是，绝望已经开始蔓延。
t_en: We've driven back the black tide, but fresh creatures continue to attack in waves. We're holding, but worse than the attacks is how despair has begun to infect our ranks.

t_ch: 来不及休息，来不及疗伤，不断看着身边的伙伴倒下，却无法收殓遗体。可是黑潮永无休止——
t_en: There's no time to rest or tend to our wounds. We watch our friends fall, unable even to retrieve their bodies. And still the black tide continues relentlessly—

t_ch: 甚至，你能在其中看到刚刚倒下的伙伴的面影。
t_en: The worst part? Sometimes you recognize the faces of your fallen comrades among these creatures.

t_ch: 抱歉…也许我不该说这些。
t_en: Sorry... I shouldn't burden you with such grim thoughts.

t_ch: 坚持到「再创世」就好了…
t_en: We just need to hold out until "Era Nova"...

t_ch: 无论是前往新的世界，还是任由{RUBY_B#死亡之泰坦}塞纳托斯{RUBY_E#}用手遮住我们的双眼，无论怎么样都好……
t_en: Whether we journey to a new world or {RUBY_B#Death Titan}Thanatos{RUBY_E#} draws his hands over our eyes... anything would be better than this endless fight...

t_ch: 刻法勒如果真的足够仁慈，它就该让这一切结束得更快一些。
t_en: If Kephale is truly merciful, they would bring this suffering to a swifter end.

t_ch: 保重。
t_en: Stay strong.

t_ch: 我们已经吹响了集结的号角，但愿更多士卒和民兵赶来后，能让我们有一丝喘息的时机。
t_en: We've sounded the call to arms. With any luck, reinforcements will arrive soon and we might finally have a moment to breathe.

t_ch: 请去帮助更多人吧，阁下。这里有我们。
t_en: Please go help the others instead. We can hold our ground here.

t_ch: 我们想要升高时，就向上仰望。有人向下俯视，因为他已经升高。
t_en: When we wish to rise, we look upward. Those who look down do so because they've already risen above.

t_ch: …没什么，只是一些对牺牲者的感想。哦，恩维尔去救他那些信{RUBY_B#死亡之泰坦}塞纳托斯{RUBY_E#}的伙伴了，所以这里只有我。那小子命大，这点变故还死不了。
t_en: ...Nothing important, just reflecting on the fallen. Avilius left to rescue his buddies — those {RUBY_B#Death Titan}Thanatos{RUBY_E#} worshipers, so I'm the only one here. That boy's as tough as they come, this chaos won't be his end.

t_ch: 你自己又在做什么？
t_en: And what exactly are you doing here?

t_ch: 思考。你有没想过，「再创世」已经发生多少次了？
t_en: Contemplating. Tell me, have you ever wondered how many times "Era Nova" has already happened?

t_ch: 这不是第一次？
t_en: This isn't the first time?

t_ch: 几十次？
t_en: Dozens of times?

t_ch: 几百万次？
t_en: Millions of times?

t_ch: 很好，你落进了我问题的陷阱里。
t_en: Perfect! You've fallen right into my trap.

t_ch: 因为我的答案其实是…无数次。
t_en: Because my true answer is... countless times.

t_ch: 啊？
t_en: What?

t_ch: 哦，你是说……
t_en: Oh, you mean...

t_ch: 我是说，既然没有经过太大的挑战，我们就可以迎来「再创世」——那它可能从根本上就是世界规则的一部分。
t_en: What I'm saying is, if "Era Nova" can arrive without tremendous struggle, then perhaps it's simply an inherent part of our world's cycle.

t_ch: 弄不好，无论我们积极努力还是消极摆烂，它都会如期到来。当然，牺牲者的规模可能完全不同，所以这两种情况也不能同日而语。
t_en: It might arrive right on schedule, whether we actively fight for it or passively accept our fate. Of course, the difference in casualties would be enormous, so these paths aren't remotely comparable.

t_ch: 只是一切难免让我猜测——翁法罗斯本来就是个循环的世界。在漫长的轮回中，人或许可以演化成泰坦，泰坦或许也可以演化成人，所有人都能经历所有人的故事。
t_en: But I can't help but speculate — what if Amphoreus is fundamentally a world of cycles? In these endless turns, perhaps mortals eventually become Titans, Titans become mortals, and everyone experiences everyone else's journey.

t_ch: 而在某一个时刻，我可以是你，你也可以是我。
t_en: And somewhere in this endless cycle, I might live as you, and you as me.

t_ch: 嚯。你像来古士老师一样一言不发。
t_en: Ah, you're giving me the silent treatment — just like Mr. Lygus would.

t_ch: 太可怕了。
t_en: That's truly disturbing.

t_ch: 想到了翁法罗斯可能根本没有未来？那的确非常可怕…不过，我们也可以试着把它变成生存的动力。
t_en: Frightened by the possibility that Amphoreus has no future? It is terrifying... but perhaps we can transform that fear into a will to survive.

t_ch: 是的，没有未来…也是一种动力。因为那也意味着没有过去。
t_en: Yes, the absence of future... is also a kind of motivation. For it also means there is no past weighing us down.

t_ch: 有点酷。
t_en: That's actually pretty cool.

t_ch: 呵呵，你和恩维尔一个反应。
t_en: Hehe, you and Avilius have the same reaction.

t_ch: 你相信「再创世」吗？
t_en: Do you believe in "Era Nova" yourself?

t_ch: 看你怎么定义「相信」。
t_en: That depends on what you mean by "believe."

t_ch: 我们一定能拥有更好的世界、过上更好的生活吗？未必。
t_en: Are we guaranteed a better world and improved lives? Not necessarily.

t_ch: 翁法罗斯人的灵魂是不灭的吗？很可能。
t_en: Are the souls of Amphoreans immortal? Quite possibly.

t_ch: 我想起了「黄金替罪羊」……
t_en: Which reminds me of the "Golden Scapegoat"...

t_ch: 「纯黑的潮汐，在岩石上留下没有名字的水。纯粹的真实之水、死亡之水——也因此没有名字。」
t_en: The ebony tide, leaving nameless water on the rocks. Pure water of truth, water of death — thus, it remains unnamed.

t_ch: 「不喝这水的人，会在水中渴死；喝了这水的人，却会失去自己的名字。」
t_en: Those who don't drink this water will die of thirst in its midst. Those who drink will lose their very names.

t_ch: 「在这映不出容貌的水中，有全世界所有人的面孔。」
t_en: In this water that mirrors no face, all the world's countenances reside.

t_ch: …的确很像是「再创世」的隐喻。
t_en: ...It certainly feels like a metaphor for "Era Nova."

t_ch: 来古士老师不是也说过？我们找到的那些句子…可能是过早翻开的答案。
t_en: Didn't Mr. Lygus say as much? Those verses we uncovered... might be answers revealed before we understood the questions.

t_ch: 那意味着，它既谎话连篇，又充满真实。
t_en: Which means it's both full of lies and full of truth.

t_ch: 也只好且行且看啦。
t_en: I suppose we'll just have to wait and see.

t_ch: 再会。
t_en: Farewell.

t_ch: 哦，再见！也许下次见面的时候，我们就认不出彼此了呢。
t_en: Oh, farewell! Maybe when we next meet, we'll be strangers to each other.

t_ch: …那倒也是有趣。
t_en: Now that would certainly be interesting...

t_ch: 又见面了，{NICKNAME}阁下。你看上去还挺精神的。找我有什么事？
t_en: Well, hello again, {M#Mr.}{F#Miss} {NICKNAME}. You're looking rather spirited. What brings you here?

t_ch: 你还活着，我很欣慰。
t_en: It's good to see you survived.

t_ch: 是啊，我还活着。说实话，起初我挺惊讶；所有被风堇姑娘用天空祝福吊住性命的人都很惊讶。
t_en: Yes, I'm still here. Frankly, I was shocked, as were all of us whose lives hung by a thread thanks to Miss Hyacine's blessings of the sky.

t_ch: 我们本以为，随着艾格勒的陨落，天空的神力会猝然失效，取而代之的是冥河的召唤。
t_en: We assumed that when Aquila fell, the sky's divinity would instantly fade, replaced by the calling of the River of Souls.

t_ch: 但是目前来看，那份来自天空的祝福并未离去，甚至…更加温暖。
t_en: But it seems the sky's blessing hasn't left us. In fact, it feels... even warmer now.

t_ch: 也许，这是留在天空的风堇姑娘，给病人们最后的礼物吧。
t_en: This must be Miss Hyacine's farewell gift to the afflicted, bestowed from her new home in the sky.

t_ch: 现状如何？
t_en: How are things going right now?

t_ch: 并不乐观。黎明云崖到处是黑潮的恶兽，战士们拼死也只能搏出寥寥几处安全区。
t_en: Far from good. Dawncloud is crawling with black tide creatures, and our warriors are dying just to secure a few safe zones.

t_ch: 外加「金织」女士离世、万帷网崩溃，战线间信息传递相当迟缓，给我们的战斗带来了相当大的麻烦。
t_en: And with Lady Goldweaver gone, and the World Wound Web collapsed, information barely travels between battle fronts, creating considerable difficulties for our efforts.

t_ch: 现在，也只能走一步看一步了。
t_en: For now, we can only take it one step at a time.

t_ch: 不过，多亏了阁下的援手，现在安全区的范围有所扩大，我们也能得到更多喘息的空间。翁法罗斯迎来新生前，一切还是不能大意。
t_en: However, thanks to your help, the safe zones have expanded somewhat, giving us more breathing room. Still, we mustn't lower our guard until Amphoreus is reborn.

t_ch: 万敌他……
t_en: And Mydei...

t_ch: 不死的迈德漠斯牺牲了——目睹了那一幕的圣城卫士已为我送来了悲报。
t_en: The undying Mydeimos has fallen — Holy City Guards who witnessed his end have delivered this tragic news to me.

t_ch: 他并未如以往那样再度站起，而是如一尊光荣的雕像，在沉默中倒塌。
t_en: He did not rise again as he always had in the past, but instead fell silent like a majestic statue crumbling.

t_ch: 阁下，收起你的哀容吧——迈德漠斯必不愿见此。
t_en: Please, cast aside your grief — Mydeimos would not want you to grieve.

t_ch: 悬锋人的礼仪，便是将一切软弱的哀怨与仇债，在战场上讨回。
t_en: The Kremnoan way is to settle all weakness, grief, and grudges on the battlefield.

t_ch: 想找你翻译一块石板。
t_en: I need your help to translate a slate.

t_ch: 悬锋古语么？给我看看吧，我姑且还算精通。
t_en: Ancient Kremnoan language? Show me — I'm rather well-versed in it.

t_ch: …呼。拿去吧。别问我，也别多话。你就把石板上记载的往事，当作无人知晓的故事吧。
t_en: Hmm... Here. Ask nothing and speak nothing of it. Treat the history recorded on this slate as a story known to none.

t_ch: {RUBY_B#负世之泰坦}刻法勒{RUBY_E#}，回应你的子民啊…为何弃我们于黑暗？我曾为你献上无数祭品，日夜诵念祷词…可如今，你在哪里？
t_en: {RUBY_B#Worldbearing Titan}Kephale{RUBY_E#}, answer cries of your people! Why have you left us in darkness? I have offered I have offered, prayed day and night... but where are you now?

t_ch: 又一位迷途者？不不不！哈哈，不过是和我一样，又一个被抛弃的可怜虫罢了！
t_en: Another lost pilgrim? No, no! Haha, just another forsaken soul, cast aside like me!

t_ch: 你还好吗？你好像很激动。
t_en: Are you alright? You seem agitated.

t_ch: 好？哈！我能有什么不好？不过是被我侍奉一生的泰坦抛弃了而已！
t_en: Alright? Ha! What could possibly be wrong? I've only been abandoned by the Titan I've devoted my entire life to!

t_ch: 你不是刻法勒的祭司吗？
t_en: You're a priest of Kephale, aren't you?

t_ch: 噢？怎么，你曾听过我的布道？那你一定知道我过去强调过多少次要「恪守祭仪」了。
t_en: Oh? You've heard my preaching? Then you must remember how often I stressed the importance of "observing the rituals."

t_ch: 我只是路过，不打扰你了。
t_en: I'm just passing by. I'll leave you now.

t_ch: 走吧，走吧！就像{RUBY_B#负世之泰坦}刻法勒{RUBY_E#}抛弃我们一样！
t_en: Go then, go! Just as {RUBY_B#Worldbearing Titan}Kephale{RUBY_E#} abandoned us all.

t_ch: 真是讽刺，那些虔诚的信徒…他们冒着生命危险把我从坍塌的神像下救出来，喊着「救救祭司大人」。
t_en: The irony! Those faithful followers... risking their lives to pull me from beneath the fallen statue, shouting "Save the high priest."

t_ch: 可笑啊，若{RUBY_B#负世之泰坦}刻法勒{RUBY_E#}真有神力，何须这些凡人来救我？
t_en: How absurd! If {RUBY_B#Worldbearing Titan}Kephale{RUBY_E#} truly held divine power, why would mortal hands be needed to save me?

t_ch: {RUBY_B#负世之泰坦}刻法勒{RUBY_E#}，你究竟是为何要抛下我们！为什么你会无动于衷啊！
t_en: {RUBY_B#Worldbearing Titan}Kephale{RUBY_E#}, why have you deserted us! How can you stay silent!

t_ch: 为何要抛下我们！为什么无动于衷啊…又是你？你来干嘛，和我一样寻找那个失踪的泰坦吗？
t_en: Why desert us! Why stay silent— You again? What are you doing here, looking for the missing Titan like me?

t_ch: 刻法勒它究竟怎么了？
t_en: What's really happened to Kephale?

t_ch: 在天灾降临之时，我曾向{RUBY_B#负世之泰坦}刻法勒{RUBY_E#}祈祷，我献上最珍贵的收藏作为祭品，可换来的…只有突然倒塌的神像与它的沉默。
t_en: When the calamity struck, I prayed to {RUBY_B#Worldbearing Titan}Kephale{RUBY_E#}, offering my most precious collections as sacrifices, but all I got in return... was a crumbling statue and silence.

t_ch: 信徒们冒着生命危险把我救出来，喊着「救救祭司大人」。真是可笑…一个无法换来神明回应的祭司，有什么值得救的？
t_en: The followers risked their lives to rescue me, shouting "Save the high priest." How absurd... what's the point of saving a priest who can't even get his god to answer?

t_ch: 自那以后，据我所知，我和其他同僚，还有其他信众们再也没得到过它的回应。这究竟算什么？是它抛弃了我们…抑或是…它遭遇了什么？我也不知道……
t_en: Since then, neither I, my fellow priests, nor any followers have heard anything from them. What does this mean? Have they abandoned us... or has something happened to them? I simply don't know...

t_ch: 那些仍然信奉{RUBY_B#负世之泰坦}刻法勒{RUBY_E#}的人…他们说这只是一次考验，说刻法勒从未离去…如此纯粹的信仰，而我居然嘲笑过他们的天真。
t_en: Those who maintain their faith in {RUBY_B#Worldbearing Titan}Kephale{RUBY_E#}... they insist this is just a test, that Kephale never left... such unwavering belief, and I had the audacity to mock their innocence.

t_ch: 我曾在元老院面前大言不惭地宣扬祭仪的力量，号召人们跟随我的指引，但现在只剩下这片废墟和无尽的疑问……
t_en: There was a time when I preached before the Council of Elders about ritual power, demanding others follow my guidance. Now all I have are ruins and endless questions...

t_ch: 你现在有什么打算？
t_en: So what's your plan now?

t_ch: 打算？我这把老骨头还能有什么打算？我倒是想一走了之，可信徒们把我从神殿里救了出来，我总不能跟{RUBY_B#负世之泰坦}刻法勒{RUBY_E#}一样抛弃他们。
t_en: Plans? What plans are left for these old bones? I wish I could simply walk away, but the faithful saved me from the temple. I can't abandon them as {RUBY_B#Worldbearing Titan}Kephale{RUBY_E#} abandoned us.

t_ch: 他们想继续祈祷，那我就带头做点祭仪，至于回应…哼！这些信众也需要我来安抚，眼下，也只能这样了。
t_en: If they wish to continue their prayers, I'll perform the rituals. As for responses... bah! These followers need my comfort as well. For now, this is our only course.

t_ch: 还是不打扰你了。
t_en: Maybe I should leave you now.

t_ch: 走吧，走吧！就像{RUBY_B#负世之泰坦}刻法勒{RUBY_E#}抛弃我们一样。
t_en: Go then, go! Just as {RUBY_B#Worldbearing Titan}Kephale{RUBY_E#} abandoned us all.

t_ch: …若你能得到这位负世之神的回应，请帮我告诉它——塞若涅斯还在等它的回应，我们需要一个解释！
t_en: If you ever get a response from the Worldbearing Titan, tell them this — Cyrones still waits for their answer. We deserve an explanation!

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，怎么还在这儿闲逛，不会是…放心不下我吧？
t_en: {NICKNAME}, still hanging around? Don't tell me.... you're worried about me?

t_ch: 你感觉好些了吗？
t_en: How are you feeling?

t_ch: 身子感觉好多了，精神更是好得不得了！
t_en: Physically, I'm much improved, and my spirits couldn't be higher!

t_ch: 听完我的事迹之后，大家看我的眼神都不一样了。呵呵，那些溢美之词有些言过其实，但还是听得人心里暖洋洋的。
t_en: Now that folks have heard my story, they see me in a new light. Hehe, some of their praise might be a bit over the top, but it still feels good.

t_ch: 说起来，克拉特鲁斯那家伙还邀请我指导这里的防卫任务呢，真拿他没办法。
t_en: Believe it or not, Krateros invited me to oversee defense operations here. What am I going to do with that fellow?

t_ch: 不过，我理解。能力越大责任越大，我应当背负的责任，实在是太沉重了！
t_en: But I get it. With great power comes great responsibility, and the burden I must shoulder is truly tremendous!

t_ch: 关于刚才的战斗…
t_en: About the battle we just had...

t_ch: 多亏了那两位小贩，没有他们的牺牲，我们守不住圣殿。
t_en: We have those two merchants to thank — without their sacrifice, the temple would have fallen.

t_ch: 他们真的很有信念感。
t_en: They had such convictions.

t_ch: 无论如何，也太莽撞了。
t_en: Regardless, they were too reckless.

t_ch: 恐怕他们只是觉得好玩而已…
t_en: I think they just did it for the thrill...

t_ch: 即便灵魂真的会去往再创世后的世界，但这一刻，死亡带来的恐惧和伤痛仍是真实的。
t_en: Even if souls really do travel to the world after Era Nova, the fear and pain of death at that moment remain real.

t_ch: 不管出于什么原因，也不管用了什么方式…豁出性命，让更多人免于死亡的折磨，都是一件高尚的事。
t_en: Whatever their reasons, whatever their methods... sacrificing one's life to spare others from the torment of death is a noble deed.

t_ch: 但愿再创世后，他们真的能够站上奥赫玛小贩业的顶点。到那时候，你也一定要去看看「奥凯纳斯运动会」的盛况啊！
t_en: I hope that after Era Nova, they truly rise to become the finest merchants in Okhema. When that happens, you absolutely must witness the grandeur of the Okeanas Games!

t_ch: 商贩汇聚，征战不休的城邦也放下兵戈。街巷、圣殿的廊柱都挂满鲜花，说着不同城邦方言的人们支起帐篷，赞美彼此健美的身体，角逐月桂花环……
t_en: The merchants convene, and constantly warring city-states pause their conflicts. Streets and temple columns are adorned with flowers, people speaking different dialects pitch tents, praise each other's athletic bodies, and compete for laurel wreaths...

t_ch: 我要坐第一排。
t_en: I'll take a front-row seat.

t_ch: 别忘了我的致谢词。
t_en: Don't forget to mention me in your speech.

t_ch: 呵呵…一定……
t_en: Hehe... of course...

t_ch: 你好好休息吧。
t_en: You should get some good rest.

t_ch: 唉，我得赶紧养好伤。继续休息下去也不是个事啊，有那么多人盼着我出场呢！
t_en: *sigh* I need to heal fast. I can't just lie around forever — there are so many people counting on me to show up!

t_ch: 英雄阁下……
t_en: Hero...

t_ch: 你还好吗？
t_en: Are you alright?

t_ch: 不怎么好，但也没差到哪去。
t_en: Not great, not terrible.

t_ch: 快去避难吧！
t_en: Hurry and seek shelter!

t_ch: 我，我不能走。有些大家伙们被吓坏了…得有人来照顾它们。
t_en: I—I can't go. Some of these big fellas are startled... Someone needs to take care of them.

t_ch: 其，其实刚刚出现怪物的时候，我怕得要死。本想跟着人流一起逃跑，但刚跑出去没多远，就看到有一只大地兽为了保护民众受了重伤……
t_en: A-actually, when the monsters appeared, I was really scared. I originally wanted to flee with the crowd, but I didn't get that far, only to see a dromas suffering major wounds while protecting the people...

t_ch: 回过神来，我就已经站在驿站这里了，手里拿着全套的疗伤用具……
t_en: When I came too again, I found myself standing here at the relay post with a full set of medical equipment at the ready...

t_ch: …对，细想一下，还是这里比较安全，不仅有着吉奥里亚的庇佑，大家伙们也会保护我的。
t_en: ...Right. Come to think of it in detail, this place is pretty safe. It's not only protected by Georios, but the big ones will also be watching over us.

t_ch: 周围的大地兽发出了呜嗡声，听起来像是在肯定这位勤恳寻土人的想法。
t_en: The surrounding dromases hum in agreement, as if they are in agreement with this diligent Earth Scout's thoughts.

t_ch: 别慌，再创世……
t_en: Don't panic. The Era Nova...

t_ch: 就快了…对吧？我不担心这个，但我想知道，大地兽们…对它们而言，新世界究竟是什么？
t_en: Is coming... right? I don't worry about that, but... what's the new world going to be like... going to be for the dromases?

t_ch: 听说，伟大的刻法勒会确保新世界中每一个生灵都得到公平对待。这是不是代表…大地兽有可能不再是大地兽，而是变成奇美拉、海豹，或是别的什么？
t_en: I heard that the great Kephale will ensure every being in the new world is treated fairly. So... does that mean dromas might turn into chimeras, seals, or something else entirely?

t_ch: 又或者，我们变成大地兽，大地兽变成我们？那听上去也不错就是了…唉，吉奥里亚在上，一定要有大地兽的容身之处啊！
t_en: Or maybe we'll turn into dromas, and they'll turn into us? That doesn't sound too bad, actually... Ahh, by Georios above, may there always be a place for the dromas!

t_ch: 接下来有什么打算？
t_en: So what's your plan now?

t_ch: 去也没地方去了，我刚刚悼念过了同僚们，之后打算专注于大家伙们身上。前段时间发明的几道新菜色，趁现在都给它们做来尝尝。
t_en: I've got nowhere to go. I just paid my respects to my fallen comrades, so after that... I'll be focusing on these big guys. Came up with a few new recipes recently — might as well let 'em try out now.

t_ch: 来吧伙计们，新鲜的红土刺身——
t_en: Come on, fellas! Fresh raw redsoil —

t_ch: 英雄阁下，你要是无处可去，不如也呆在这吧。吉奥里亚伴随着我们，没事的，都会没事的……
t_en: If you've got nowhere else to go, hero, why not stay here with us? With Georios watching over us, everything's going to be okay... It's going to be okay...

t_ch: 英雄阁下，我必须向你致歉……
t_en: Hero, I must apologize to you...

t_ch: 老先生，不必如此多礼。
t_en: Sir, there's no need for that.

t_ch: 为什么要道歉？
t_en: What are you apologizing for?

t_ch: …从重渊离开时，我对黄金裔与救世预言尚存疑虑。而现在，我只自责于自己的愚昧。
t_en: ...When I left the Abyss, I still doubted the Chrysos Heirs and the prophecy of deliverance. Now, I only blame myself for my ignorance.

t_ch: 信仰种种，需要生者来延续价值——直至目睹生命的消散，我方才明白。
t_en: Faith holds meaning when the living carry it forward — only after witnessing the loss of life did I finally understand.

t_ch: 如果世界能够重生，那一定是因为有人可以走到道路的尽头。
t_en: If the world is to be reborn, it will be because someone could walk all the way to the end of the road.

t_ch: …维尔图斯……
t_en: ...Virtus...

t_ch: 「谁来背负灵魂的重，换取世人步伐之轻……」
t_en: "Who then will bear the weight of humanity's souls, to lighten the steps of the living as a whole..."

t_ch: 希望，先一步行至西风尽头的他不会孤单。
t_en: Having gone ahead of us to the end of west wind, I hope he won't feel alone.

t_ch: 您现在对再创世怎么看？
t_en: What do you think of the Era Nova now?

t_ch: 它会到来，一定会。无数的牺牲之后，必定能有人到达终点。然后，让它成为起点。
t_en: It will come. It must. After countless sacrifices, someone will reach the end. And from there, it will be a new beginning.

t_ch: 无数历史，无数纷争，无数消亡，都为此刻而生。现在，我坚信这一点。
t_en: Countless histories, endless strife, lives lost beyond measure — all were meant for us to reach this moment. Now, I believe that with all my heart.

t_ch: 还请多保重。
t_en: Take care of yourself.

t_ch: 谢谢，年轻的勇士。愿命运…眷恋于你。
t_en: Thank you, young warrior. May fate... favor you.

t_ch: 文明的痕迹该如何跨越时空，留给千百年后的人类？
t_en: How can the traces of civilization transcend time and space, so that people a thousand years from now may find them?

t_ch: 你怎么站着不动？
t_en: Why are you standing still?

t_ch: 你听说过多利安古城吗？在黄金战争的年代，这座古城被喷发的火山吞没，漫天的火山灰，顷刻间将整座城邦封存于时光的琥珀中——
t_en: Ever heard of the ancient city of Doria? During the Chrysos War, a volcanic eruption swallowed the city whole. And in an instant, a cloud of ash sealed an entire civilization in the amber of time —

t_ch: 建筑体、墙壁上镌刻的誓言，甚至尚未腐败的碗中食物……透过这些遗迹，我们得以窥见千年前的多利安文明的辉煌与温度。
t_en: Its structures, the oaths carved into its walls, even bowls of untouched food... Through these ruins, we glimpse the brilliance and warmth of the Dorian culture, frozen in time.

t_ch: 如今，末日的舞台被搬到奥赫玛…若我还有时间，我该如何为后人传递文明的信号？
t_en: And now, Okhema has become the stage of the apocalypse... If I still have time, how should I leave a message for those who come after us?

t_ch: 这些弥足珍贵的…世界毁灭的线索，黄金裔救世的壮举，文明点滴的幸福……
t_en: Such precious information... clues on the destruction of the world, the heroic deeds of the Chrysos Heirs as they try to save the world, the fleeting joys of a civilization...

t_ch: 你不相信再创世吗？
t_en: You don't believe in the Era Nova?

t_ch: 正如我们初见时我所说，我无比期盼翁法罗斯的明天……
t_en: As I told you when we first met, I long for Amphoreus's tomorrow...

t_ch: 但这并不是因为我恐惧失败——欧洛尼斯令时间的长河奔流不止，即使你我毁灭，也会浇灌出下一个文明。
t_en: But it's not because I fear failure — Oronyx keeps the river of time flowing. Even if we fade away, a new civilization will eventually bud again.

t_ch: 比起灵魂，我更在意文明的传承。
t_en: I care more about the legacy of civilization than the soul.

t_ch: 你的收藏们怎么样了？
t_en: How's your collection coming along?

t_ch: 你果真了解我，比起担忧这条命，我更牵挂那些岁月的见证。
t_en: You really do know me. Instead of worrying about my life, I care more about those testimonies of times lived.

t_ch: 哎，可没人清楚末日会以怎样的灾难袭来，该采用何种保存物件的对策，我也毫无头绪……
t_en: Ah, but no one knows what kind of disaster the end of the world will bring, or what methods we should use to save objects. I'm at a loss too...

t_ch: 我觉得最有可能的情况是…抬头看这火烧的天空啊！仿佛下一秒，这火球就要将刻法勒的背脊折断，坠入人间。
t_en: I think the most likely scenario is... just look at this burning sky! It feels like the fireball will snap Kephale's back and fall to the ground at any moment.

t_ch: 或许…我应当赶到黎明机器照拂不及的偏远城邦，将宝物埋在那里。
t_en: Maybe... I should head to a remote city, far from the Dawn Device's reach, and bury the treasures there.

t_ch: 例如雅努萨波利斯！虽然黑潮四伏，但它们肯定对我这些破玩意儿不感兴趣。
t_en: Like Janusopolis! Sure, it's swarming with the black tide, but they definitely won't care about my old junk.

t_ch: 希望你一切顺利。
t_en: I hope everything goes smoothly for you.

t_ch: 嗯，你也多保重呀。
t_en: Take care of yourself as well.

t_ch: 对了，若遇见白厄阁下，也请将我的祝福带给他。
t_en: By the way, if you happen to see Lord Phainon, please give him my regards.

t_ch: 女孩望着你，一言不发，但她的眼中似乎透露着一种期盼。
t_en: The girl looks at you, silent, but there's a glimmer of hope in her eyes.

t_ch: 你好，小妹妹。
t_en: Hello, little one.

t_ch: 有什么需要帮忙的吗？
t_en: Is there anything I can help with?

t_ch: 女孩试着用手势与你「交谈」，虽然你不懂手语，不过迷迷接替了翻译的职责——
t_en: The girl tries to communicate with you through gestures. You don't understand sign language, but Mem steps in to translate —

t_ch: （{F#大姐姐}{M#大哥哥}，我见过金发的姐姐，和你在一起。）
t_en: ({F#Miss}{M#Sir}, I've seen a blonde lady before. She was with you.)

t_ch: （她…离开了吗？）
t_en: (Did... did she leave?)

t_ch: …别担心，她没事。
t_en: ...Don't worry, she's fine.

t_ch: 再创世后，一定还能再见的。
t_en: We'll definitely see her again after the Era Nova.

t_ch: 女孩似乎能读懂你的唇语，她沉默了半晌，好像有些难过。
t_en: The girl seems to understand your lip movements. She's silent for a while, looking a little sad.

t_ch: （对不起，可能是我的错……）
t_en: (I'm sorry, maybe it's my fault...)

t_ch: 女孩的眼睛一闪一闪，似乎欢迎着你的到来。
t_en: The girl's eyes sparkle, as if welcoming your presence.

t_ch: 能和我说说发生什么了吗？
t_en: Can you tell me what happened?

t_ch: （几天前，有一些可怕的人闯进我家里。我看到，他们说悄悄话，如果姐姐不消失，就要取走我的眼睛。）
t_en: (A few days ago, some scary people broke into my house. I saw them whispering, and they said that if that lady didn't disappear, they'd take my eyes.)

t_ch: （那帮混账……！）
t_en: (Those fiends...!)

t_ch: （连这么小的孩子都……！）
t_en: (They won't spare even a little kid...!)

t_ch: （…倒是符合他们的人设。）
t_en: (...Well, but it does fit their character.)

t_ch: （我很害怕，但他们向我解释说，没关系，我没有做坏事，而且为了我，姐姐一定会消失。）
t_en: (I was scared, but they explained that it's okay, I haven't done anything bad, and that the lady will definitely disappear for me.)

t_ch: （看上去…他们也很为难，一个人说，这件事是领导他们的人决定的。姐姐她，一定很困扰吧……）
t_en: (They seemed... quite troubled. One of them said it was the decision of their leader. That lady... she must have been troubled too...)

t_ch: （不过，有一只紫色的精灵抱着宝箱路过，在那些人离开前，它好像一直在暗中想保护我。所以，我没事的。）
t_en: (But a purple sprite holding a chest passed by, and it seemed like it was trying to protect me in secret while those people were here. So, I'm fine.)

t_ch: 做坏事的人已经得到报应了。
t_en: The bad people have already gotten what they deserve.
(What they deserve...)

t_ch: （…嗯，这样一来，就不会有人欺负姐姐了，对吧。）
t_en: (...Umm, now no one will bully that lady anymore, right?)

t_ch: 还有什么我能帮上忙的吗？
t_en: Is there anything else I can help you with?

t_ch: （{F#大姐姐}{M#大哥哥}，如果你还能见到金发姐姐，能替我向她道歉吗？）
t_en: ({F#Miss}{M#Sir}, if you'd ever see the blonde lady again, can you apologize to her for me?)

t_ch: 我会的。
t_en: Sure thing.

t_ch: 别担心，这不是你的错。
t_en: Don't worry, this isn't your fault.

t_ch: （但…要是我能保护自己的话，姐姐就不会被欺负了，所以，拜托你。）
t_en: (But... if I could protect myself, that lady wouldn't have been bullied. So, please...)

t_ch: （谢谢你，{F#大姐姐}{M#大哥哥}。）
t_en: (Thank you, {F#miss}{M#sir}.)

t_ch: 保护好自己，下次见。
t_en: Take care of yourself, and I'll see you next time.

t_ch: （嗯，再见，{F#大姐姐}{M#大哥哥}！愿刻法勒祝福你！）
t_en: (I will. Goodbye, {F#miss}{M#sir}! May Kephale bless you!)

t_ch: 秘酿末日特价——不要<unbreak>300</unbreak>，不要<unbreak>200</unbreak>…全场免费，我请客！
t_en: Ambrosia's apocalyptic discount: Not <unbreak>300</unbreak>, nor <unbreak>200</unbreak>... it's all free! My treat!

t_ch: 我也来一杯。
t_en: I'll have one.

t_ch: 当然！也别搞什么随机秘酿了，我给老顾客备了最好的。
t_en: Of course! And don't go for the random ambrosia. I've got the best reserved for regulars.

t_ch: 来试试这瓶千年的陈酿，从法吉娜最后的庆典上保存至今，我一直没舍得动。
t_en: Come, try this bottle of millennium-aged brew. It's been preserved since the last celebration of Phagousa. I haven't been able to bring myself to open it until now.

t_ch: 来，与我一同举杯！一杯敬过去快活的日子，一杯敬永不到来的明天！
t_en: Come, let's raise a toast! One glass to the happy days gone by, and another to the tomorrow that will never come!

t_ch: 你捧起瓶身，一饮而尽！可预想中的迷醉之意并未到来……
t_en: You raise the bottle, and down it in one go. But the expected intoxication doesn't hit...

t_ch: 一点没感觉是吗…呵呵，你的神经紧绷，战胜了法吉娜的神力。
t_en: No feeling at all, huh? Heh, your nerves are so tense that you've resisted Phagousa's divine power.

t_ch: 看来，你并不属于这里。你注定要奔赴更伟大的使命，我的朋友。
t_en: Looks like you don't belong here. You're destined for a greater mission, my friend.

t_ch: 振作，再创世要成功了！
t_en: Cheer up, the Era Nova is about to succeed!

t_ch: 再创世意味着什么，我搞不明白。
t_en: What does the Era Nova mean? I don't understand.

t_ch: 但，连永不熄灭的黎明机器都撑不住了，我看这就是翁法罗斯的终点了……
t_en: But even the ever-burning Dawn Device can't hold up anymore. I think this is the end of Amphoreus...

t_ch: 我害怕…浴场的大伙们害怕…没有人不害怕……
t_en: I'm scared... the people at the baths are scared... no one isn't scared...

t_ch: 那就痛饮吧！现在我感觉好多了，身上暖暖的，那种惴惴不安也消失了。
t_en: Then drink up! I feel much better now. I feel warm, and that nervousness has disappeared.

t_ch: 至少，这最后一次，我终于听到{RUBY_B#法吉娜}我的神明{RUBY_E#}对我说话了，它说——
t_en: At least, for this last time, I finally heard {RUBY_B#Phagousa}my deity{RUBY_E#} speak to me. They said —

t_ch: 「赶紧喝吧，等天真塌下来，你手头上好的秘酿就都浪费了」，哈哈哈！
t_en: "Hurry and drink up. When the sky truly collapses, your good ambrosia will all be wasted," hahaha!

t_ch: 尽情享受吧。
t_en: Enjoy yourself.

t_ch: 嗯，也祝你好运，我的朋友。
t_en: Yeah. And good luck to you, my friend.

t_ch: 若真有来世，愿世界依偎在{RUBY_B#法吉娜}它{RUBY_E#}的{RUBY_B#海洋}摇篮{RUBY_E#}中……
t_en: If there really is an afterlife, I hope the world will rest in {RUBY_B#Phagousa's}their{RUBY_E#} {RUBY_B#Ocean}cradle{RUBY_E#}...

t_ch: 哎，英雄阁下，我问你——到了新世界，你有什么想做的？
t_en: Hey, hero, I have a question for you — what do you plan to do in the new world?

t_ch: 危机还在，没空想这些。
t_en: The crisis is still here. No time to think about that.

t_ch: 这…是我冒昧了，不过命运泰坦在前，我们也总该向前看嘛。
t_en: Well... I may be overstepping, but the Fate Titans are ahead, and we should always look forward.

t_ch: 想当大地兽。
t_en: I want to be a dromas.

t_ch: 您居然和老师开了同样的玩笑…莫非这是某种黄金裔之间才懂的暗喻？
t_en: You actually made the same joke as my teacher... could this be some kind of metaphor only understood between the Chrysos Heirs?

t_ch: 新世界？什么新世界？
t_en: New world? What new world?

t_ch: 好家伙，您怎么比我还疯啊。
t_en: Good heavens, you're crazier than I am.

t_ch: 瞧我，等到了新世界，我得先去找那刻夏老师重新拜个师，要是他答应，那我可就成了泰坦的学生了！哈哈，再要找工作岂不是平步青云！
t_en: When we get to the new world, the first thing I'm going to do is find Professor Anaxa and beg him to take me as his disciple again. If he agrees, I'll be a student of a Titan! Haha, finding a job after that would be smooth sailing!

t_ch: 你好像完全没有危机感嘛。
t_en: You really don't seem to have a sense of crisis.

t_ch: 你不知道，金织女士的事件之后，元老院饱受非议，我也被人指指点点的，这班简直上够了。
t_en: There's something you don't know. After Lady Goldweaver's incident, the Council of Elders has been heavily criticized, and even I've been pointed at. This job I have right now is just beyond my tolerance.

t_ch: 我脑子会不停蹦出「世界毁灭吧」的念头，结果想着想着，天就真黑了……
t_en: My mind kept thinking, "The world should end," and before I knew it, the sky turned dark...

t_ch: …抱歉，这玩笑的确有些不合时宜。其实，我刚也经历了九死一生，现在满脑子只想着快点迈入新世界，好和家人朋友们重逢。
t_en: ...Sorry, that joke was a bit out of place. I went through such a bad experience that all I think about now is stepping into the new world and reuniting with my family and friends.

t_ch: 不如说，我只能坚信再创世是事实，所有人都会迈入轮回，然后表现得一派轻松。不然，真要是陷入自我怀疑…我可能会撑不下去。
t_en: If anything, I must believe that the Era Nova is real and that everyone will step into the cycle, then act as if it's nothing. Otherwise, if I truly fall into doubt... I might not be able to carry on.

t_ch: 好耶，我也想去找老朋友。
t_en: Yay, I also want to meet some old friends.

t_ch: 可不是！哈哈，我跟在这里的人已经决定了，要笑着迎接新世界的到来，这样就不会被先一步离开的人调侃了。
t_en: Exactly! Haha, those of us here have already decided to welcome the new world with a smile. That way, we won't be mocked by those who left before us.

t_ch: 那个，谢谢你，英雄阁下，我听说了，我们的命都算是你救的。
t_en: Thank you, hero. I've heard that we owe our lives to you.

t_ch: 可能我有点多管闲事，但…如果撑不住了，别硬撑，大家随时都愿意跟你聊聊。
t_en: Maybe I'm being a busybody, but... if you can't hold on anymore, don't force yourself. We're always here if you need someone to talk to.

t_ch: 啊，阁下你还有正事，我就不多耽误你的时间了。请多保重，我们新世界再会吧！
t_en: Ah, I think you might have important matters to attend to. I won't take up more of your time. Please take care. We'll meet again in the new world!

t_ch: 是你啊，贵客…如今阿格莱雅大人已死，其他位置的衣匠们也都进入了自主模式。
t_en: It's you, honored guest... Now that Lady Aglaea is dead, the other Garmentmakers have gone into autonomous mode.

t_ch: 阿格莱雅大人…她一直想留下些不会被黑潮冲刷的东西，就像把字刻在石头上。哪怕我们无法战胜黑潮，也不该让存在的证明就此消失。
t_en: Lady Aglaea... she always wanted to leave something that the black tide couldn't wash away, like carving words into stone. Even if we couldn't defeat the black tide, we shouldn't let proof of our existence disappear.

t_ch: 如果若虫数量再多一些的话，或许她就能成功了…可要去哪儿找这么多若虫呢？
t_en: If only there were more nymphs, maybe she could have succeeded... but where would we find so many?

t_ch: …贵客，我们来继续搜集若虫吧。
t_en: ...Honored guest, let's continue gathering nymphs.

t_ch: 或许这已经没有意义…但它是我现在唯一能做的事。
t_en: Perhaps it no longer matters... but it's the only thing I can do now.

t_ch: 如果你愿意帮我，我会十分感激…带着阿格莱雅大人的份一起。
t_en: If you're willing to help, I'd be very grateful... and so would Lady Aglaea.

t_ch: 在这最后的时刻，您仍愿意去满足一个虚掷的愿望…不胜感激。
t_en: In these final moments, you're still willing to fulfill a pointless wish... I can't thank you enough.

t_ch: 哪里还有没搜集的若虫？
t_en: Where are the remaining nymphs?

t_ch: 衣匠能顺着金丝找到它们的踪迹，但这需要消耗<color=#dbc291ff>「记忆残晶」</color>才能做到。
t_en: The Garmentmaker can follow the golden thread to ascertain their whereabouts, but this requires consuming <color=#dbc291ff>Memory Crystal Shards</color>.

t_ch: 提供「记忆残晶」。
t_en: Provide Memory Crystal Shards.

t_ch: 衣匠一把抓住记忆残晶，顷刻炼化。
t_en: The Garmentmaker grabs the shards and instantly refines them.

t_ch: 嗯…有位织者重视情谊，不愿与旧友们分离。金丝带来消息，它正在<color=#dbc291ff>大地兽站点的铃铛上</color>，目送奔赴战场的人们呢。
t_en: Hmm... There is a weaver who values connections deeply and cannot bear to say goodbye to friends. The golden thread brings tidings: They now reside <color=#dbc291ff>on the bell of Dromas Station</color>, watching the people heading to the battlefield.

t_ch: 嗯…有位织者不满于美的凋零，总想为人们添加一分亮色。金丝带来消息，它正在<color=#dbc291ff>城墙外道的花坛边</color>，为匆匆离去的友人担心呢。
t_en: Hmm... There is a weaver unsatisfied with the fading of beauty, who wishes only to add a touch of brightness to others. The golden thread brings tidings: They are at <color=#dbc291ff>the flowerbed by the city wall's outer path</color>, worrying about the friends who are leaving in a hurry.

t_ch: 嗯…有位织者沉迷于浴池带来的乐趣。金丝带来消息，它正在<color=#dbc291ff>浴场的中央水池</color>中，不知骚乱是否打扰了它的雅兴？
t_en: Hmm... There is a weaver absorbed in the pleasure brought by the bath. The golden thread brings tidings: They are at <color=#dbc291ff>the central pool of the baths</color>. I wonder if the chaos has interrupted their enjoyment?

t_ch: 嗯…有位织者醉心于黄金血的甜美，就像悬锋战士沉迷于蜜酿。金丝带来消息，它正在<color=#dbc291ff>褔坛的金池中央</color>，希望逃遁进另一个世界呢。
t_en: Hmm... There is a weaver who is mesmerized by the sweetness of Golden Blood, like a Kremnos Warrior addicted to honey brew. The golden thread brings tidings: They now reside <color=#dbc291ff>in the center of the Golden Pond at the Altar of Blessings</color>, hoping to escape into another world.

t_ch: 嗯…有位织者想要把自己锤炼得像山之民一样坚韧。金丝带来消息，它正在<color=#dbc291ff>铁匠的火炉上</color>锻炼耐性呢。快让它知难而退吧。
t_en: Hmm... There is a weaver who aspires to be as resilient as the Mountain Dwellers. The golden thread brings tidings: They now reside <color=#dbc291ff>on the blacksmith's forge</color>, honing their endurance and patience. Hurry and retrieve them before they hurt themselves.

t_ch: 嗯…有位织者想要让世人品尝甜美。金丝带来消息，它正把自己伪装成一块喂食大地兽的红土，你可以在<color=#dbc291ff>大地兽工坊</color>附近找到它。
t_en: Hmm... There is a weaver who wishes for all lives to experience a touch of sweetness. The golden thread brings tidings: They now disguise themselves as a piece of redsoil that nourishes the dromas. You may find them near the <color=#dbc291ff>Dromas Workshop</color>.

t_ch: 嗯…有位织者通晓各种偏门知识。金丝带来消息，它正藏在<color=#dbc291ff>云石天宫的升降梯井</color>，把自己伪装成了墙壁的一部分。要抓住时机才能见到它。
t_en: Hmm... There is a weaver who possesses knowledge of all unorthodox sorts. The golden thread brings tidings: They now secretly reside <color=#dbc291ff>in the Marmoreal Palace's elevator shaft</color>, disguising themselves as part of the wall. You need to catch the right opportunity to see them.

t_ch: 嗯…有位织者跃跃欲试、渴望证明自己的速度。金丝带来消息，它正停在<color=#dbc291ff>一张桌子上</color>，等待着挑战者上门呢。
t_en: Hmm... There is a weaver who jumps at the chance and is eager to prove its speed. The golden thread brings tidings: They now rest <color=#dbc291ff>on a table</color>, waiting for new challengers.

t_ch: 嗯…有位织者总想成为法吉娜的圣兽。金丝带来消息，它把自己藏在了<color=#dbc291ff>云石天宫的圣兽摊位</color>，去那里找找看吧。
t_en: Hmm... There is a weaver who dreams of becoming the sacred beast of Phagousa. The golden thread brings tidings: They now hide <color=#dbc291ff>within the Sacred Beast Stall in Marmoreal Palace</color>. Seek them there.

t_ch: 嗯…有位织者向往传说、渴望和英雄们一起冒险。金丝带来消息，它正伏在<color=#dbc291ff>一幅壁画上</color>，向画中蝶诉说心中困惑呢。你得擦亮眼睛才能发现它。
t_en: Hmm... There is a weaver who yearns for legends and adventures alongside heroes. The golden thread brings tidings: They now linger <color=#dbc291ff>within a mural</color>, telling the painted butterfly about their confusion. You must sharpen your eyes to spot them.

t_ch: 嗯…有位织者向往高处、希望像纷争世的帝王那样手握权力。金丝带来消息，它正站在<color=#dbc291ff>俯瞰浴场的高处</color>，发表豪言壮语呢。
t_en: Hmm... There is a weaver who aspires to great heights, longing to wield power like an emperor of Era Bellica. The golden thread brings tidings: They now stand <color=#dbc291ff>at the peak overlooking the baths</color>, making grand speeches.

t_ch: 嗯…有位织者因亲族失散而心怀不安。金丝带来消息，它就在<color=#dbc291ff>我们身边</color>，希望你的到来能带给它安慰。
t_en: Hmm... There is a weaver feeling uneasy because of losing contact with their family. The golden thread brings tidings: They are <color=#dbc291ff>near us</color>, hoping your arrival will bring them comfort.

t_ch: 嗯…有位织者自诩博古通今。金丝带来消息，它正在<color=#dbc291ff>云石天宫的休息室内</color>锐评一位学者的演讲，你可以去指教一二。

t_ch: 嗯…有位织者在艾格勒的怒火下幸存。金丝带来消息，它正在<color=#dbc291ff>通往黄金池的廊道</color>边，怕是在暗自庆幸吧。
t_en: Hmm... There was a weaver who survived Aquila's wrath. The golden threads brought news, it is now <color=#dbc291ff>by the corridor leading to the Golden Pond</color>, probably secretly relieved.

t_en: Hmm... There was a weaver who loved nourishing their body in baths. The golden threads brought news, it's currently soaking in <color=#dbc291ff>a golden pond</color>. Go ask how the experience is.

t_en: Hmm... There was a weaver who had just set out on their journey. The golden threads brought news, it's trapped under <color=#dbc291ff>a pile of fallen rubble</color>. Let's hope this doesn't discourage them.

t_ch: 嗯…有位织者独自踏上了求索之旅。金丝带来消息，它正沿着<color=#dbc291ff>纹路光带</color>前行，不知它又会有怎样的体悟呢？
t_en: Hmm... There was a weaver who embarked alone on a journey of seeking. The golden threads brought news, it is now following a <color=#dbc291ff>patterned light band</color>. I wonder what kind of enlightenment it will find this time?

t_en: Hmm... There was a weaver with an impatient temperament. The golden threads brought news, it's at the <color=#dbc291ff>top of the lift shaft</color>, fuming over the tardy lift platform.

t_en: Hmm... There was a weaver who faithfully carried out its duty for millennia. The golden threads brought news, it now waits before the <color=#dbc291ff>gate to the celestial mural</color>. It's time to call it back to walk among us once more.

t_ch: 嗯…有位织者见惯了冬之民城邦的凛冬。金丝带来消息，它正伏在<color=#dbc291ff>一个通风口上</color>，试图用寒风吹去它的汗珠呢。
t_en: Hmm... There is a weaver who is accustomed to the harsh winters of the Winterfolk city-states. The golden thread brings tidings: They are resting on <color=#dbc291ff>a ventilation shaft</color>, trying to blow away their sweat with the cold wind.

t_ch: 嗯…有位织者熟悉晖之民的信仰。金丝带来消息，它正仰望着<color=#dbc291ff>坍塌的穹顶</color>，念诵着耳濡目染的祷告呢。
t_en: Hmm... There was a weaver well-versed in the faith of the Sunfolk. The golden threads brought news, it now gazes up at the <color=#dbc291ff>collapsed dome</color>, reciting prayers learned through lifelong devotion.

t_ch: 嗯…有位织者与伙伴结伴同行。金丝带来消息，它们正在<color=#dbc291ff>一堆储存器元件上</color>，希望它们面对黑潮威胁能同心协力。
t_en: Hmm... There was a weaver traveling with companions. The golden threads brought news, they're now atop <color=#dbc291ff>a pile of storage unit components</color>. May they stand united against the threat of the black tide.

t_ch: 嗯…有位织者怀揣着惩恶扬善的信念。金丝带来消息，它正在<color=#dbc291ff>一根石柱上</color>，为雨之民打抱不平呢。
t_en: Hmm... There is a weaver who carries the belief of punishing evil and promoting good. The golden thread brings tidings: They are on <color=#dbc291ff>a stone pillar</color>, standing up for the Rainfolk.

t_ch: 嗯…有位织者苦练技艺，终于小有所成。金丝带来消息，它正在<color=#dbc291ff>天象画壁前的悬台高处</color>，去检验一番它的成果吧。
t_en: Hmm... There is a weaver who has diligently honed their skills and has finally made progress. The golden thread brings tidings: They are at <color=#dbc291ff>a high place of the suspended platform in front of the celestial mural</color>, ready to test its achievements.

t_ch: 嗯…有位织者登高望远，却被黑潮困住。金丝带来消息，它正在<color=#dbc291ff>能看到彩虹桥的悬台上</color>，请去助它一臂之力吧。
t_en: Hmm... A weaver climbed high to gaze afar, only to be trapped by the black tide. The golden threads bring news, it's now on <color=#dbc291ff>a platform overlooking the rainbow bridge</color>. Go lend it your aid.

t_ch: 嗯…有位织者好为人师。金丝带来消息，它正在<color=#dbc291ff>描绘壁画的长廊上</color>，等待为过路人解惑呢。
t_en: Hmm... There is a weaver who is eager to teach others. The golden thread brings tidings: They are on <color=#dbc291ff>the corridor with murals</color>, waiting to enlighten passersby.

t_ch: 嗯…有位织者渴望一段赤诚的友谊。金丝带来消息，它正在<color=#dbc291ff>井道的壁面上</color>，寻找同行的伙伴呢。
t_en: Hmm... There is a weaver who yearns for a sincere friendship. The golden thread brings tidings: They are on <color=#dbc291ff>the well shaft's wall</color>, looking for a companion.

t_ch: 嗯…有位织者对建筑修筑颇有研究。金丝带来消息，它正躲藏在<color=#dbc291ff>穹顶的石柱上</color>，仰起头或许能发现它的踪迹。
t_en: Hmm... There was a weaver who studied architecture intently. The golden threads brought news, it's now hiding among <color=#dbc291ff>the pillars of the dome</color>. Look up, you might just spot it.

t_ch: 不给~
t_en: Nope~

t_ch: 抱歉，艾格勒并不欢迎金丝的探访，没有记忆残晶，金丝无法违逆它的权柄。
t_en: Sorry, Aquila does not welcome the visit of the golden thread. Without the Memory Crystal Shard, the golden thread cannot defy their authority.

t_ch: 正有一只若虫等着你将它唤回呢，请先去把它找回来吧。
t_en: There's a nymph waiting for you to call it back. Go find it and bring it home first.

t_ch: 我找齐了这里的所有若虫。
t_en: I've found all the nymphs here.

t_ch: 能多给我点提示吗？
t_en: Can you give me more hints?

t_ch: 我去忙了。
t_en: I'll get back to work.

t_ch: 天空的火种既被夺取，无法预料这里还会发生什么…贵客，请务必小心。
t_en: The Sky Coreflame have been stolen, who knows what may yet befall this place... Honored guest, do take care.

t_ch: 风儿顺着金丝捎来了你的消息。贵客，可有什么进展吗？
t_en: The winds carried your message along the golden threads. Honored guest, any progress to share?

t_ch: 我似乎已经集齐了石板。
t_en: It seems I've gathered all the stone tablets.

t_en: Let me see... That's right, distinguished guest, this is the entire manuscript of the prophet. The golden thread can extract more information from it.

t_ch: 现在，或许是时候向你揭露阿格莱雅大人计划的全貌了。
t_en: Now, perhaps it's time to reveal the full scope of Lady Aglaea's plan to you.

t_ch: 它始于一个渺小又崇高的理想：抵御黑潮。准确的说，从中活下来。
t_en: It began with a small-yet-noble ideal: to resist the black tide. To be precise, to survive it.

t_ch: 贵客早已知晓黑潮对翁法罗斯大地的摧残，无论人还是奇兽，都无法从它的影响下幸存——唯有一物例外。
t_en: The esteemed guest is already aware of the devastation the black tide has wrought upon the land of Amphoreus. Neither humans nor strange beasts can survive its influence, only one thing is an exception.

t_ch: 是…若虫？
t_en: Is it... nymph?

t_ch: 没错，相信贵客应该也见识过，织者能够短暂抵御黑潮的侵袭。虽不能在其中自由活动，但黑潮确实无法将它们彻底吞噬。
t_en: That's right. I believe the esteemed guest has also witnessed that the weavers are able to temporarily resist the invasion of the black tide. While they cannot move freely within it, the black tide indeed cannot completely engulf them.

t_ch: 这，便是从无到有的第一步。若能探明织者之秘背后的原理，终有一天，翁法罗斯的人民将不再畏惧天灾。
t_en: This is the first step from nothing to something. If we can uncover the secrets behind the weavers' mystery, one day, the people of Amphoreus will no longer fear natural disasters.

t_ch: 但根据眼下的测算，计划所需的若虫远远多于我们所能找到的。究竟该从哪里取得剩下的若虫，我也没有头绪。
t_en: But according to the current calculations, the number of nymphs required for the plan is far greater than what we can find. I have no idea where to obtain the remaining nymphs.

t_ch: 无论如何，感谢你一直以来的协助。
t_en: Regardless, thank you for your continued assistance.

t_ch: 感谢你，贵客。现在，我会着手解封众多预言中的一则。
t_en: Thank you, esteemed guest. And now, I shall begin to unfurl one of the many prophecies.

t_en: Golden threads gather around the mannequin, forming a cocoon that envelops its body.

t_ch: 当茧褪去时，唯余一块金丝织成的锦缎漂浮在空中。衣匠伸手一挥，它便分作两半，其中一半飞进了你的手里……
t_en: When the cocoon fades, only a piece of golden brocade remains floating in the air. The Garmentmaker waves a hand, and it splits in two, with one half flying into your grasp...

t_ch: <color=#dbc291ff>「汝需一路向东；西风会赐你一双捷足，勿让这份祝福消失。」</color>
t_en: <color=#dbc291ff>"You must head east. The west wind shall grant you swiftness — do not squander its blessing."</color>

t_en: Once, a messenger ran for three days and three nights without rest, only to collapse and die, all to deliver news of victory to the holy city.

t_en: But now, we have no victory to report. Only a city slowly crumbling to ruin...

t_ch: 或许，你也该跑起来了，贵客。
t_en: Perhaps it's your turn to start running, honored guest.

t_ch: 我注意到你已经获取了预言所指的目标。在业已崩坏的奥赫玛，果然发生什么都不奇怪……
t_en: I see you've obtained the object foretold in the prophecy. In a place as shattered as Okhema, nothing should come as a surprise...

t_ch: 总之，感谢你的协助，这是我仅剩的一点薄礼，还请收下。
t_en: Either way, thank you for your help. This is a small token of my gratitude. Please accept it.

t_en: A strange prophecy... Hard to say if it's a good omen or not. Perhaps you should examine this goldweaver more closely, esteemed guest.
